title: veronica anagallis-aquatica subsp. undulata
intro: Veronica anagallis-aquatica subsp. undulata, also known as water speedwell, is a subspecies of the water speedwell plant, which is a type of flowering perennial that grows in wetlands and aquatic environments. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Veronica anagallis-aquatica subsp. undulata include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
